rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND THROUGH VS. OAKDALE MOB, A CRIMINAL STREET GANG SUED AS AN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion To Modify Permanent Injunction Related To Oakdale Mob [California Civil Code §3424, California Code Of Civil Procedure §533]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 28, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, Notice Of Motion To Modify Permanent Injunction Related To Oakdale Mob [California Civil Code §3424, California Code Of Civil Procedure §533] Plaintiff People of the State of California/s motion to modify permanent injunction related to Oakdale Mob is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SPECIALIST STAFFING SOLUTIONS, INC. VS. SPECIFIC TECHNOLOGIES LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of The First Cause Of Action Of The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 28, 2018, Line 8, PLAINTIFF SPECIALIST STAFFING SOLUTIONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of The First Cause Of Action Of The Complaint. Plaintiff Specialist Staff Solutions, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of the first cause of action of the complaint is denied. There is a triable dispute whether the parties' contract required plaintiff to submit a suitable candidate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant Specific Technologies LLC is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KWAN WO IRONWORKS INC. VS. THOMPSON BUILDERS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Thompson Builders Corporation And Berkley Insurance Companys Notice Of Motion To Compel Contractual Arbitration And Motion To Stay The Action [Code Civ. Proc., 1281, 1281.2, 1281.4]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 28, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANTS BERKLEY INSURANCE COMPANY Thompson Builders Corporation's Notice Of Motion To Compel Contractual Arbitration And Motion To Stay The Action [Code Civ. Proc., 1281, 1281.2, 1281.4] Defendants Thompson Builders Corporation and Berkley Insurance Company's motion to compel arbitration and to stay the action is granted. The Thompson/Kwan Wo contract contains an enforceable provision for binding arbitration and is not unconscionable. Except for the provisions of the DRB manual that are specifically referenced in the contract, the DRB manual is not applicable to the resolution of the parties' disputes. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the moving parties is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
